grail.canonicalQuery:
fetch dt.davis.problems, from:now() - 2h
| fields event.id, display_id, event.status, event.start, event.end, event.category, event.description, affected_entity_ids, related_entity_ids
| filter if("ACTIVE" == "ALL", TRUE, else:event.status == "ACTIVE")
| sort event.start
| limit 200
grail.query:
fetch dt.davis.problems, from:now()-2h
| fields event.id, display_id, event.status, event.start, event.end, event.category, event.description, affected_entity_ids, related_entity_ids
| filter if("ACTIVE" == "ALL", true, else:event.status == "ACTIVE")
| sort event.start
| limit 200

notifications.messageFormat: The expression `%1$s` is always false because the  two arguments of %2$s are not equal.
notifications.message: The expression `"ACTIVE" == "ALL"` is always false because the  two arguments of == are not equal.

tool.name: query-problems
tool.title: Root Cause Agent
tool.description:
Query all Davis Problems.
When querying Davis problems, it's important to understand their lifecycle and how to filter results effectively.
Within a problem lifecycle, each problem has a start time. If the end time is not set, the problem is considered ACTIVE. Once an end time is defined, the problem is considered CLOSED.
Only the 200 most recent problems are returned.

includeTypes.description: If true the DQL query result types will be included in the result. Default value is true.

history.description:
Specifies the time range in minutes (m), hours (h), or days (d) to look back when querying Davis problems:
problems with a timestamp in the range [now-history, now] are returned.
Integer hours to look back from now (e.g., 30m = last half an hour, 5h = last 5 hours, 2d = last 2 days, 7d = last week)
**DO NOT** use values greater than 60 days unless explicitly requested by the user.

status.description:
the status of the problem to query. The value can be one of the following values:
ACTIVE, CLOSED or ALL.
If ALL is provided then both ACTIVE and CLOSED problems are returned.
The default value is ALL

outputSchema.description: Result of executing a DQL query against Dynatrace Grail

records.description: The records returned by the DQL query.

types.description: The types for the records returned by the DQL query.

metadata.description: Metadata about the query execution and results from Grail

outputSchema.title: Grail DQL Query Result

_meta.customerFacingDescription: Explore the 200 most recent problems, active or closed.

parts.text: ⚡ Quick triage: no suspect commits found within 60 minutes before the incident. The nearest commit was ea294f89 by Sai, more than 12 hours before incident. Confidence: LOW.
